setup_task.sh: 8 articles (lines 112-211) + 7 cases (lines 214-278) = 15 total items. Verified via Python script analysis.

data_provenance.description: All 15 items (8 law review articles and 7 court cases) are synthetically generated in setup_task.sh via Python script. The articles and cases represent real, published legal works with accurate citations, but they are not downloaded from external sources. Instead, they are hardcoded into the setup script and inserted into the Juris-M SQLite database for testing purposes.

real_sources_verified.item: A Taxonomy of Privacy by Daniel J. Solove
Broken Promises of Privacy by Paul Ohm

privileged_info_summary: The task involves a Juris-M library containing 15 real, published legal works: 8 law review articles (with 4 missing abstracts) and 7 landmark court cases primarily addressing Fourth Amendment and privacy law. The parent collection is named 'Tech & Privacy Special Issue' with three required subcollections: 'Data Privacy Articles', 'Digital Search Cases', and 'Surveillance Law'. All items must be tagged with 'tech-privacy-2024' and the Quick Copy citation format must be set to Chicago Manual of Style 17th edition (full note). A standalone 'Table of Contents' note listing all 15 items must be created. The underlying data is synthetically generated by seeding the Juris-M database with these real legal publications during task setup.
